user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to start a new project using specified inputs, so that the project is created and placed in the appropriate mode.
true_user_story: The START NEW PROJECT function shall allow the user to create a new project.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be prompted to provide the new project name and the low level document path when creating a new project, so that all necessary information is collected for proper project setup.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the new project name.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be prompted to provide the new project name, low level document path, high level document path, and RTM file path, so that all necessary information for setting up the project is collected accurately.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the low level document path.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be prompted to provide the new project name, low level document path, high level document path, and RTM file path, so that all necessary information is collected for setting up the project.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the high level document.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to select an IR Method from the list including Vector-Space Retrieval, Keyword Extraction, and Keyword Extraction-IDF, so that I can choose the most appropriate method for my information retrieval needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select an IR Method from the following list: Vector-Space Retrieval Keyword Extraction Keyword Extraction-IDF

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either “Both Documents” or “Low Level Document Only”, with the default value being “Both Documents”, so that I can customize my keyword extraction process according to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either “Both Documents” or “Low Level Document Only”.  The default value is “Both Documents”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a "Weight Option" from the list: TF-IDF (Default), Okapi, LTU, so that I can customize my search results based on different weighting algorithms.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide Weight Option from the following list: TF-IDF (Default) Okapi LTU

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select a 'Weight Threshold' when I choose an IR Method of 'Keyword Extraction' or 'Keyword Extraction-IDF', so that the system can accurately process my input.
true_user_story: If the user has selected an IR Method of Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction-IDF, the user must also select a Weight Threshold.

user_story: As a user, I want to optionally provide a Thesaurus filename and a Stop Word filename, so that I can customize my text processing experience.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a Thesaurus Filename.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a Thesaurus Filename and a Stop Word file, so that I can customize my search parameters according to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the Stop Word file.

user_story: As a user, I want to optionally provide a Feedback Method, such as 'Standard Rochio', so that I can customize my feedback experience.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a Feedback Method.  “Standard Rochio” is currently the only option.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to cancel the process and return to the previous state at any time before the project is created, so that I can avoid unwanted actions or changes.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the process and return to the previous state at any time before the project is created.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to create a new project using default input fields and select the appropriate mode (Trace, Project Mode, or Assess) so that the project is correctly initialized according to the specified requirements.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create the project using the above input.  The project shall be placed in “Trace” mode.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to load an existing project, so that I can continue working on it from the last saved state with all associated mode options.
true_user_story: Since the majority of projects are not completed in a single setting, then it is critical to provide the user the option to load an existing project to continue working on the project.  Load Project feature will provide the user the capacity to open an existing project in the last saved state with all the associated mode options. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select a project name from a list of saved projects, so that I can load an existing project or create a new one.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to select a project name from a list of saved projects.  Project names are obtained by scanning the saved projects directory.

user_story: As a user, I want to optionally provide a Feedback Method, with 'Standard Rochio' being the only available choice, so that my feedback can be effectively processed.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to select a feedback method.  The only option is “Standard Rochio”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to cancel the LOAD PROJECT action and return to the previous activity, so that I can avoid loading an incorrect project or change my mind about the action.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the LOAD PROJECT action and return to the previous activity.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to cancel the LOAD PROJECT action and return to the previous activity, so that I can avoid loading an incorrect project and continue with my work without interruption.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to load the project once the project name and feedback method is selected.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to load an existing RTM project, so that I can continue working on it without having to start from scratch.
true_user_story: Similar in functionality to the START NEW PROJECT function, the LOAD RTM function collects project parameters from the user and initializes the project.  A project created via the LOAD RTM function may be in either “trace” or “assess” mode.  Assess mode is usually used when the user is provided a RTM and needs to evaluate its accuracy, completeness, and satisfaction level.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be prompted to provide the new project name and the low level document path when creating a new project, so that all necessary information is collected for proper project setup.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the new project name.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be prompted to provide the new project name, low level document path, high level document path, and RTM file path, so that all necessary information for setting up the project is collected accurately.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the low level document path.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be prompted for the high level document path and RTM file path, so that I can accurately input the necessary files for the new project setup.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the high level document path.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide the high-level document path and the RTM file path, so that I can accurately input the necessary files for processing.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the RTM file path.

user_story: As a user of RETRO, I want to be optionally prompted to select an IR Method from the list including Vector-Space Retrieval, Keyword Extraction, and Keyword Extraction-IDF, so that I can choose the most appropriate method for my information retrieval needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select an IR Method from the following list: Vector-Space Retrieval Keyword Extraction Keyword Extraction-IDF

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either “Both Documents” or “Low Level Document Only”, with the default value being “Both Documents”, so that I can customize my keyword extraction process according to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either “Both Documents” or “Low Level Document Only”.  The default value is “Both Documents”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a "Weight Option" from the list: TF-IDF (Default), Okapi, LTU, so that I can customize my search results based on different weighting algorithms.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide “Weight Option” from the following list: TF-IDF (Default) Okapi LTU

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select a 'Weight Threshold' when I choose an IR Method of 'Keyword Extraction' or 'Keyword Extraction-IDF', so that the system can accurately process my input.
true_user_story: If the user has selected an IR Method of “Keyword Extraction” or “Keyword Extraction-IDF”, the user must also select a “Weight Threshold”.

user_story: As a user, I want to optionally provide a Thesaurus filename and a Stop Word filename, so that I can customize my text processing experience.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a “Thesaurus” filename.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide filenames for the Thesaurus and Stop Word files, so that I can customize my experience by using specific word lists.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the “Stop Word” filename.

user_story: As a user, I want to optionally provide a Feedback Method, with 'Standard Rochio' being the only available choice, so that my feedback can be effectively processed according to the system's capabilities.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a “Feedback Method”.  “Standard Rochio” is currently the only option.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select either 'Trace' or 'Assess' mode when starting a new project, with 'Trace' being the default option, so that I can choose the appropriate mode based on whether I am creating a new project or evaluating an existing one.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the “Project Mode” of either “Trace” or “Assess”.  “Trace” is the default mode.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to cancel the process and return to the previous state at any time before the project is created, so that I can avoid unwanted actions or changes.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the process and return to the previous state at any time before the project is created.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to create a new project using specified inputs and place it in either 'Project Mode', 'Assess' mode, or 'Trace' mode, so that I can effectively manage the project lifecycle according to its current stage.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create the project using the above input.  The project shall be placed in specified “Project Mode”.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to save the current data and status of my project using the SAVE function, so that all progress is preserved and can be retrieved later.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to activate the SAVE function to save the current data and status of the project.

user_story: As a user, I want RETRO to save all current data and project when activated, so that my work is not lost.
true_user_story: When activated RETRO will save all current data and project.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to close my current project without exiting the RETRO application, so that I can save my work and continue using other features of the application.
true_user_story: The CLOSE CURRENT PROJECT Function will allow the user to close a given project without exiting the RETRO application.

user_story: As a user of the RETRO application, I want to be able to close the current project without exiting the application, so that I can save my progress and return to it later.
true_user_story: The user shall be able close the current project.

user_story: As a user, I want to exit the RETRO application when I am finished working with it, so that I can close all my projects and end my session cleanly.
true_user_story: It is necessary to provide a mechanism to exit the RETRO application when the user is finished working with the application.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to exit the RETRO application when I am finished working with it, so that I can close the application and free up system resources.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to exit the application.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to view the current project’s XML file format, so that I can understand and verify the structure of the project data.
true_user_story: The user may want to view the current project’s XML file format.  The SHOW XML function will provide the user a display of the current XML file.

user_story: As a user, I want to view the XML version of the RTM, so that I can inspect and validate its structure.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to view the XML version of the RTM.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to view a list of reports generated after the completion of a trace or assess process, so that I can review and analyze the project outcomes.
true_user_story: After a project has completed either a trace or an assess process, then the user will need the capability to review the final results of the process in an easy to read format other than the XML file format that is automatically generated throughout the process.  The GENERATE REPORT function will provide the user a list of reports that the user may view.  The user will have the option to save the generated report or modify the design of the report. [1]

user_story: As a software user, I want to generate and save an RTM report with text option from the Action menu, so that I can have detailed documentation of my activities.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “RTM report”

user_story: As a system administrator, I want to generate and save various reports including 'Parents with no children', 'Children with no parents', 'RTM report', and 'Parent with no children report', so that I can monitor and manage the data effectively.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Parent with no children report”

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to generate and save various reports including 'Parents with no children with text report', 'Children with no parents report', 'Children with no parents with text report', and 'Not satisfied requirements report'. This will allow me to analyze specific data sets effectively. Additionally, the 'Parents' report should open in a new screen.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Parents with no children with text report”

user_story: As a data analyst, I want to be able to generate and save various reports including 'Parents with no children', 'Children with no parents', 'RTM report', 'Parent with no children report', 'Not satisfied requirements report', so that I can effectively monitor and analyze the data for any discrepancies or missing information.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Children with no parents report”

user_story: As a data analyst, I want to be able to generate and save reports for various relationship scenarios such as 'Parents with no children', 'Children with no parents', 'Children with no parents with text', and 'Not satisfied requirements'. This will allow me to effectively analyze and document the relationships within our dataset. Additionally, I need to access these reports through a dedicated screen or menu option.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Children with no parents with text report”

user_story: As a project manager, I want to generate and save reports for unsatisfied requirements with text details, so that I can effectively track and address any unmet project needs.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Not satisfied requirements report”

user_story: As a project manager, I want to generate and save reports for unsatisfied requirements with text, so that I can easily identify and address any gaps in our project documentation.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Not satisfied requirements with text report”

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to filter and limit the display of low-level requirements or candidate requirement links, so that I can focus on relevant information and ensure that all necessary links are included without any missing low-level requirements.
true_user_story: “The user shall be able to limit the display of low-level requirements or candidate requirement links.” [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to designate whether the filter process applies to the selected high level document element (LOCAL) or to all high level document elements (GLOBAL), so that I can control the scope of the filtering process effectively.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to designate whether the filter process applies to the selected high level document element (LOCAL) or to all high level document elements (GLOBAL).

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to filter the list of low-level requirements by setting a weight cutoff point between 0 and 1.0, so that I can limit the display of low-level requirements or candidate requirement links based on their relevance.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to filter the list of low-level requirements by setting a cutoff point for the weight between 0 and 1.0.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to select and display the top N elements by choosing the "Show Top" radio option and entering a positive number in the textbox, so that I can easily view the most relevant items.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to designate that the top N elements to be displayed.

user_story: As a user, I want to activate the Kill Hidden Links function, so that all elements not currently displayed are removed from the view.
true_user_story: The user shall be able activate a KILL HIDDEN Links function to remove all elements not currently displayed.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to sort low-level requirements by 'Requirement ID' and 'Weights', so that I can efficiently organize and prioritize the tasks based on their importance and sequence.
true_user_story: The user will need the ability to view the text of low-level requirements.  The DISPLAY function will provide three different sort options to view the text of low-level requirements.  The user can sort the low-level requirements based upon the weight of linking relationships, original document order, or by requirement id. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to sort the low level documents by "Requirement ID", so that I can easily organize and find specific requirements.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Requirement ID”

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to sort low-level documents by 'Requirement ID', 'Weights', and 'Document Order', so that I can efficiently organize and prioritize the tasks and requirements within my projects.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Weights”

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to sort low-level documents by "Document Order", "Requirement ID", and "Weights" so that I can easily organize and find specific information within the documents.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Document Order”

user_story: As a user of RETRO, I want to be able to switch between "Trace" and "Assess" modes, so that I can choose the appropriate mode for my needs.
true_user_story: Since RETRO’s tracing and assessing modes are based upon algorithms that will allow processes to be improved as the system receives FEEDBACK from the user.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to select a low-level requirement and identify its link status from the options: Link, No Link, Default. Additionally, I want to easily remove low-level requirements with a 'No Link' status by right-clicking on them and selecting 'Change -> Remove'. This will help me efficiently manage multiple projects and their associated requirements.
true_user_story: The user shall select a low level requirement and identify the link status from the following list: Link No Link Default

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to load and work on multiple projects simultaneously, so that I can efficiently manage my workload and switch between tasks without losing progress.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to load multiple projects at the same time.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to load and manage multiple projects simultaneously in the RETRO System, so that I can efficiently switch between different tasks and maintain their last saved states with all associated mode options. Additionally, I need the CLOSE CURRENT PROJECT Function to allow me to close a specific project without exiting the entire application, ensuring seamless workflow continuity.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall allow multiple projects to be open at the same time

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to import projects into the system, so that I can easily start working on new initiatives without having to manually create each project from scratch.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to import projects.

user_story: As a RETRO System user, I want the system to prompt me for file paths for high-level and low-level requirements, as well as the RTM file path, so that I can accurately input the necessary files for processing.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall provide a mechanism to convert existing input text files into RETRO file format.  (Not implemented.)

user_story: As a user, I want a graphical user interface (GUI) to interact with the RETRO system, so that I can easily navigate and perform tasks without any complexity.
true_user_story: The user shall use a GUI INTERFACE to work with RETRO.

user_story: As a user, I want to interact with the RETRO System using a GUI interface, so that I can easily and efficiently perform tasks without needing to understand complex commands or scripts.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall provide a GUI mechanism for the user to interact with the RETRO System.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to enter keywords into a search text box in BROWSE mode, so that I can quickly locate low-level requirements or high-level requirements related to my search.
true_user_story: The user is provided the capability via BROWSE mode to manually link low-level requirements to high-level requirement. In order to help the user to quickly locate the desired low-level requirement or high-level requirement, a KEYWORD SEARCH function is offered to the user. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to enter a keyword into a search text box to search within both low-level and high-level requirements, so that I can quickly locate the desired requirement.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to enter a keyword into a search text box to search within low-level requirements and the system shall return matches.

user_story: As a user, I want to enter a keyword into a search text box to search within both high-level and low-level requirements, so that I can quickly locate the desired requirement.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to enter a keyword into a search text box to search within high-level requirements and the system shall return matches.

user_story: As a user of RETRO, I want to be able to manually link/trace items in BROWSE mode, so that I can override RETRO’s suggested links when they are not accurate.
true_user_story: There will be times when the user will not agree with RETRO’s list of candidate links.  Therefore, it is important to provide a mechanism for the user to manually change, create, or delete links between low and high level requirements.  BROWSE mode is the mechanism within RETRO that enables the user to perform MANUAL LINKING/TRACING. [1]

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to filter and limit the display of low-level requirements or candidate requirement links, so that I can focus on relevant information and streamline my workflow.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create and remove low-level requirements with high-level requirements.

user_story: As a software developer, I want RETRO to automatically generate a list of candidate links from high-level requirements to low-level requirements, so that I can efficiently trace and manage requirement dependencies.
true_user_story: One of the purposes of RETRO is to aid the user in linking low-level requirements with high-level requirements.  TRACING functionality is the component of RETRO that automatically generates a list of candidate links of high to low-level requirements.

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want RETRO to provide a mechanism for generating a trace on all requirements, so that I can evaluate the completeness and consistency of our requirement set.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to generate a trace on all requirements.

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want RETRO to provide a mechanism for generating a trace on all requirements, so that I can evaluate the completeness and consistency of our requirement set.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to generate a trace on a given requirement(s).

user_story: As a software user, I want to be able to provide feedback on the tracing results after the tracing process is completed, so that future tracing processes can be improved based on my input.
true_user_story: After the TRACING function is performed, then the results of the TRACING process will need to be made available to the user to view.  The TRACING RESULTS functionality will display a list of all candidate links of high to low-level requirements based on any given filtering criteria.  The user will be allowed to provide feedback on the TRACING RESULTS in order to improve future TRACING.

user_story: In order to help the user quickly identify the level of satisfaction with each candidate link, RETRO should display a list of candidate links based on tracing and permit feedback, filtering, and display functionality.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display the list of candidate links based on the tracing and permit feedback, filtering, and display functionality.

user_story: As a user, I want to save the current status of my trace result, so that I can preserve my progress and return to it later.
true_user_story: The SAVE TRACE function shall allow the user to save the trace result.

user_story: As a user, I want to save the current status of my trace, so that I can resume my work later without losing any progress.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism to save the current status of trace.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to complete the tracing process using the COMPLETING TRACE function, so that the project can be marked as completed and no further changes are permitted.
true_user_story: The COMPLETING TRACE function shall allow user to finish the tracing process.

user_story: As a project team member, I want RETRO to save all current data and projects when activated, so that we can ensure no information is lost during the process.
true_user_story: RETRO shall mark the project completed and not permit further changes.

user_story: As a software tester, I want to be able to run accuracy and completeness tests on tracing results, so that I can provide feedback to improve future tracing.
true_user_story: The user needs a mechanism that will help to determine whether the TRACING was successfully performed based on accuracy and completeness.  An accuracy test tells the user that the candidate links provided are correctly related to the high-level requirement.  Completeness test tells the user that all possible links that are listed in the candidate list are included and no low-level requirements are missing. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to run an ACCURACY & COMPLETENESS check by performing a full trace on the requirements and comparing the RETRO IR method results with the given result from the RTM, so that I can assess the accuracy and completeness of the details contained in the RTM. Additionally, recommended links will be present if there are any suggestions for improvement.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to be able to run an ACCURACY & COMPLETENESS check by performing a full trace on the requirements and comparing the IR method results to the given RTM. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to see a list of recommendations while in ASSESS mode, so that I can provide feedback on whether or not my results match the given RTM.
true_user_story: “The [user] will need to have a list of recommendations based on RETRO’s TRACING process.  SHOW RECOMMENDATION will display to the [user] a list of recommendation while in ASSESS mode.  The [user] will have the capability to provide FEEDBACK to the RETRO system on whether or not the [user] agrees with RETRO’s assessment of the linking between the low-level requirements to the high-level requirements.” [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to display and hide recommendations at my request, so that I can control what information is shown to me while using RETRO in ASSESS mode.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display/hide recommendations at the user’s request.

user_story: As a software developer, I want to implement a satisfaction analysis function that checks the percentage level of satisfaction for linked results and updates options like "Satisfied", "Not Satisfied", and "Partially Satisfied" so that high-level requirements can be accurately evaluated against given sets of low-level requirements.
true_user_story: Satisfaction function shows how satisfied the high-level requirement is with the given set of low-level requirements. [4] The Satisfaction Analysis function is checking the level of percentage for a satisfaction of the linked result with the high and low level input files. Once final output is agreed, the list of high level requirement is updated with options like “Satisfied”, “Not Satisfied” and “Partially Satisfied”. The status of high level requirement is color coded. Unsatisfied result can be repeated until all the high levels has been satisfied stage.

user_story: In order to quickly identify the level of satisfaction for each candidate link, as a user, I want RETRO to display the percentage level of satisfaction for each link when I click the Run Satisfaction button, so that I can easily assess the quality of the links.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display to the user the percentage level of link. [1]

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to remove all low-level requirements with a status of 'No Link', so that I can quickly clean up the requirement list and ensure only relevant items are included in the assessment process.
true_user_story: While in ASSESS mode, the user will need a mechanism to allow the removal of all low-level requirements that are not linked to a high-level requirement.  REMOVE ALL NO LINKS function will provide an easy way for the user to quickly remove low-level requirements with status of “No Link”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to remove all links marked as 'No Link', so that I can clean up my data and ensure only relevant links are retained.
true_user_story: RETRO shall remove all links marked as “No Link”

user_story: As a user in ASSESS mode, I want to be able to delete recommended links that I feel are not correctly linked, so that I can maintain accurate and relevant linkages.
true_user_story: While in ASSESS mode, the user will need a mechanism that will allow the deletion of recommended links that the user feels are not correctly linked.

user_story: As a user of RETRO, I want to be able to remove all links marked as 'No Link', so that I can maintain an organized and relevant list of recommended links.
true_user_story: RETRO shall permit the user to select a recommended link and delete it.

user_story: As a user, I want the system to color-code the satisfaction status of each candidate link based on its satisfaction level, so that I can quickly identify each link's satisfaction level.
true_user_story: “In order to help the user to quickly identify the level of satisfaction of each candidate link, RETRO will need to provide a visual aid.  COLOR-CODED SATISFACTION STATUS function will color code each candidate link based on its satisfaction level.   The various colors will be a visual aid to the user to help quickly identify each links satisfaction level.” [1]

user_story: As a user, I want RETRO to color-code the satisfaction status of each candidate link based on its satisfaction level, so that I can quickly identify each link's satisfaction level.
true_user_story: RETRO shall COLOR-CODE THE SATISFACTION STATUS of each given candidate link.
